report:
EcoLoom Technologies – Research Memo
Date: June 10, 2026

I. Company Overview and Business Model

– EcoLoom Technologies, Inc. is a U.S.-based advanced materials company specializing in sustainable textiles and fabrics derived from biodegradable and recycled raw materials. Founded in 2015, headquartered in Portland, Oregon.
– Private company; last funding round (Series D) completed March 2026 raising $85M at a post-money valuation of $400M.
– Core products and revenue streams:
   • BioFibra™ line: proprietary biodegradable fiber blends used by apparel brands for performance and sustainable products.
   • ReKnit™ recycled fabric line: fabrics made from 100% post-consumer textile waste.
   • Custom R&D services: materials development for fashion, automotive, and consumer goods sectors.
– Business model: B2B supplier with long-term contracts and innovation partnerships with mid to large apparel and technical textile companies.

Scale and Financial Highlights (Fiscal Year 2025):
– Revenue: $112.5M, up 82% YoY (vs $61.8M in 2024).
– Gross profit: $45.0M (40% gross margin).
– EBITDA: $8.1M (7.2% margin), up from ($2.3M) loss in 2024.
– Net income: $1.2M profit versus ($4.8M) loss in 2024.
– Cash balance: $28.3M at fiscal year-end.
– Backlog: $76M committed contracts extending into 2027.

II. Market and Competitive Landscape

A. Industry Context

– The global sustainable textile market is forecast to grow at 15-18% CAGR through 2030, driven by consumer demand, regulatory pressure, and corporate ESG commitments.
– Regulatory tailwinds: EU Microplastics Regulation (effective 2027) and U.S. Textile Waste Reduction Act (pending) boost demand for biodegradable and recycled fibers.

B. Competition

– Primary competitors include:
   • GreenWeave Fibers: established recycled materials supplier, slower innovation cycle.
   • PureVec Textiles: synthetic but fully recyclable fibers; limited biodegradability.
   • MaterEco Solutions: experimental biotech fiber startups, early-stage with limited scale.
– EcoLoom’s key differentiators:
   • Patented BioFibra™ technology offers biodegradability within 24 months without microplastic shedding.
   • Strong vertical integration from raw feedstock (agricultural waste) to finished fabrics.
   • Fast scale-up capability with two manufacturing plants commissioned in 2025.

III. Customers and Contracts

– Top 10 customers accounted for 62% of 2025 revenue; top five apparel brands and two automotive suppliers.
– Multi-year supply contracts average 3-5 years with annual volume commitments.
– Recently secured a strategic partnership with Northwind Apparel (leading sustainable outdoor brand) expanding annual supply volume by 35% starting 2026.
– Customer retention rate over 90%.

IV. Technology and Manufacturing

– Holds six key patents related to fiber composition and biodegradable processing.
– Operates two manufacturing facilities in Oregon and North Carolina with combined annual capacity of 18,000 metric tons.
– Automation extensively implemented, reducing labor costs and increasing yield percentage from 72% in 2024 to 81% in 2025.
– Supply chain secured via partnerships with agricultural waste suppliers (e.g., hemp, flax, and banana fibers).

V. Financial Overview

A. Historical Financials (in $ millions)

| Year | Revenue | Gross Profit | EBITDA | Net Income |
|-------|---------|--------------|--------|------------|
| 2023  | 35.4    | 11.8         | (7.1)  | (12.3)     |
| 2024  | 61.8    | 22.2         | (2.3)  | (4.8)      |
| 2025  | 112.5   | 45.0         | 8.1    | 1.2        |

– Solid revenue CAGR (~82% in last year), strong improvement to positive EBITDA in 2025.
– Gross margin stable around 40%, industry standard for specialty textiles.

B. Balance Sheet (as of Dec 31, 2025)

– Total assets: $98.5M
– Total liabilities: $52.1M, including $22M long-term debt (equipment financing).
– Shareholders’ equity: $46.4M
– Working capital positive at $15.3M.

C. Cash Flow

– Operating cash flow positive for first time in 2025: $5.4M.
– Capex of $18.9M for plant expansion and equipment upgrades.
– FCF negative $(12.5)M due to heavy CAPEX but expected to improve in 2026.

VI. Growth Opportunities

– Capacity expansion planned for 2027 to double annual output, meeting demand pipeline.
– New product development into automotive interior fabrics and home furnishings.
– Geographic expansion plans into Europe and Asia through partnerships and localized manufacturing.
– Upselling R&D collaboration services with existing customers and licensing of patented tech.

VII. Risks and Considerations

– Customer concentration risk given 62% revenue from top 10 clients.
– Technology risk: competitors developing alternative sustainable fibers.
– Raw material supply chain exposed to agricultural yield variability and price fluctuations.
– Capital-intensive growth path necessitates careful financing and execution.

Summary

EcoLoom Technologies is a rapidly growing advanced materials company positioned at the forefront of the sustainable textile market, benefiting from strong product innovation, solid customer contracts, improving financial performance, and favorable regulatory trends. The company is transitioning into profitability with scalable operations and a growing backlog.

---
reasoning:
Okay, looking at EcoLoom Technologies, this company operates in the sustainable textiles sector, which is currently experiencing strong growth driven by consumer preferences and regulatory pressures. Their focus on biodegradable and recycled fibers is well aligned with macro trends favoring ESG and circular economy initiatives.

Let me analyze their financial trajectory. EcoLoom grew revenue from $61.8M in 2024 to $112.5M in 2025—an 82% increase, which is impressive for a specialty materials company. Gross margins are stable at 40%, which is healthy for this industry. They turned EBITDA positive in 2025 at 7.2% margin after losses over prior years, indicating a meaningful inflection point. Net income turned slightly positive, and operating cash flow is now positive, albeit with heavy capex. The balance sheet shows manageable debt with positive equity and working capital.

Considering competition, EcoLoom stands out with their patented BioFibra™ technology offering real biodegradability without microplastic shedding, which is a material differentiator versus competitors like PureVec or early-stage startups. Their vertical integration and dual manufacturing plants also support defensibility and scalability.

Regarding customers, there is some concentration with 62% revenue from top 10 clients, but those are well-known apparel brands with multi-year contracts and strong retention (>90%), which lowers churn risk. The recent strategic partnership with Northwind Apparel and large backlog of $76M add revenue visibility.

On growth, planned capacity expansion and product line extensions into automotive and home textiles unlock clear pathways for scaling revenues. The company’s geographic expansion plans into Europe and Asia are also important growth vectors.

Risks to watch include customer concentration, raw material supply risks tied to agriculture, technology advancements by competitors, and the capital-intensity of rapid expansion. However, their strong patent portfolio and early mover advantage mitigate some technology risk.

Overall, EcoLoom appears to be a high-potential growth company in a favorable industry with improving profitability and clear expansion opportunities, justifying a strong investment conviction.

verdict.evidence_pro:
1. Rapid revenue growth (82% YoY) with transition to positive EBITDA and net income in 2025.
2. Proprietary patented BioFibra™ technology providing genuine biodegradability with no microplastic shedding.
3. Vertical integration and operational scale with two manufacturing plants and capacity expansion plans.
4. Long-term supply contracts with well-known sustainable apparel and automotive customers ensuring revenue visibility.
5. Strong market tailwinds driven by regulatory frameworks and consumer sustainability demand.
6. Positive operating cash flow achieved, demonstrating improving cash generation.
verdict.evidence_contra:
1. Customer concentration risk with 62% revenue from top 10 clients.
2. Raw material supply dependency on agricultural waste subjects company to supply variability and pricing risk.
3. Capital-intensive growth requiring significant ongoing investment and financing.
4. Competitive threat from emerging biotech fibre companies and established competitors developing alternative sustainable materials.
5. Geographic expansion execution risk as the company moves into Europe and Asia.
6. Technology obsolescence risk if new fiber technologies outperform BioFibra™.
verdict.similar_deals: GreenBridge Partners' $120M growth equity investment in NatureTex Fibers (2023), a sustainable textile manufacturer with patented bio-based fibers; EcoCapital's $85M buyout of RenewFabric Technologies in 2025 focused on recycled fabrics for automotive interiors; TerraVest's $150M growth capital into BioWeave Solutions (2024), an advanced materials company supplying biodegradable technical textiles to outdoor apparel brands.
verdict.key_information_requested: Detailed customer contract terms and renewal rates to assess future revenue visibility; raw material supply agreements and pricing to evaluate cost stability; capex plans and timelines for capacity expansion; patent portfolio strength and competitor IP landscape analysis; environmental certifications and sustainability reporting data; detailed unit economics on new product lines and geographic initiatives.
